name: Rolex
description: Rolex SA is a Swiss luxury watch manufacturer based in Geneva. Founded by Hans Wilsdorf and Alfred Davis in London in 1905, Rolex is the single largest luxury watch brand in the world. The company pioneered numerous watchmaking innovations including the first waterproof wristwatch (Oyster, 1926), the first self-winding mechanism with perpetual rotor (1931), and the first wristwatch to show the date (Datejust, 1945). Rolex produces approximately 1 million watches per year, each assembled by hand in their Geneva facilities.

name: Omega
description: Omega SA is a Swiss luxury watchmaker based in Biel/Bienne. Founded by Louis Brandt, Omega has been the official timekeeper of the Olympic Games since 1932 and was the first watch worn on the Moon during the Apollo 11 mission in 1969. Known for the Speedmaster, Seamaster, and Constellation collections, Omega combines heritage with cutting-edge technology including their Co-Axial escapement and Master Chronometer certification.

name: Patek Philippe
description: Patek Philippe SA is a Swiss luxury watch and clock manufacturer founded in 1839. Widely regarded as the most prestigious watch manufacturer in the world, Patek Philippe remains family-owned by the Stern family since 1932. The company holds over 100 patents and is known for producing the most complicated mechanical watches ever made, including the Grandmaster Chime with 20 complications.

name: Audemars Piguet
description: Audemars Piguet is a Swiss luxury watchmaker based in Le Brassus in the Vallée de Joux. Founded by Jules Louis Audemars and Edward Auguste Piguet, it is the oldest fine watchmaking company still in the hands of its founding families. The Royal Oak, designed by Gérald Genta in 1972, revolutionized luxury watchmaking by introducing the concept of a luxury steel sports watch.

name: Cartier
description: Cartier International SNC is a French luxury goods conglomerate that designs, manufactures, and sells jewellery, leather goods, and watches. Founded in Paris in 1847, Cartier created one of the first practical wristwatches — the Santos — in 1904 for aviator Alberto Santos-Dumont. The Tank, introduced in 1917, remains one of the most iconic watch designs ever created.

name: Seiko
description: Seiko is a Japanese watchmaker founded in 1881 by Kintarō Hattori. Seiko revolutionized the watch industry by producing the world's first quartz wristwatch (Astron, 1969) and the first GPS solar watch. Known for exceptional value and in-house manufacturing capabilities, Seiko produces everything from affordable mechanical watches to high-end timepieces.

name: Grand Seiko
description: Grand Seiko is the luxury watch brand of Seiko Group, originally launched in 1960 with the goal of creating the ideal watch. Known for the Spring Drive movement — a unique hybrid of mechanical and electronic technology — and for dials inspired by Japanese nature. Grand Seiko's finishing standards rival those of the finest Swiss manufacturers at significantly lower prices.

name: TAG Heuer
description: TAG Heuer is a Swiss luxury watchmaker known for its sports watches and chronographs. Founded by Edouard Heuer in 1860, the brand has deep roots in motorsport timing and was the first Swiss watch in space (1962). TAG Heuer pioneered several chronograph innovations including the oscillating pinion and the first automatic chronograph movement (Calibre 11, 1969).

name: IWC Schaffhausen
description: International Watch Company (IWC) is a Swiss luxury watch manufacturer located in Schaffhausen. Founded by American watchmaker Florentine Ariosto Jones, IWC uniquely combines Swiss craftsmanship with American engineering. Known for the Portugieser, Pilot's Watch, and Portofino collections, IWC is renowned for robust tool watches and clean, legible designs.

name: Jaeger-LeCoultre
description: Jaeger-LeCoultre is a Swiss luxury watch and clock manufacturer founded in 1833 in the Vallée de Joux. Known as the 'watchmaker's watchmaker,' the Manufacture has created over 1,400 different calibres and holds over 400 patents. Its most iconic creation, the Reverso, features a reversible case originally designed to protect the crystal during polo matches.

name: Tudor
description: Tudor is a Swiss watch brand founded by Hans Wilsdorf, the founder of Rolex. Created to offer Rolex quality at a more accessible price point, Tudor has evolved into a brand with its own distinct identity. The Black Bay collection has become one of the most popular dive watch lines in modern horology, and Tudor now produces its own in-house movements.

name: Longines
description: Longines is a Swiss luxury watchmaker based in Saint-Imier. Founded in 1832, it is one of the oldest watch brands still in existence. Longines has a rich heritage in aviation, exploration, and sports timing. The brand is known for elegant designs and excellent value within the accessible luxury segment.

name: Tissot
description: Tissot is a Swiss watchmaker founded in 1853 in Le Locle. As part of the Swatch Group, Tissot produces innovative and accessible Swiss watches. The brand pioneered tactile watches with the T-Touch collection and is the official timekeeper of numerous sports including MotoGP, the NBA, and the Tour de France.

name: Citizen
description: Citizen Watch Co. is a Japanese watchmaker known for pioneering Eco-Drive technology, which converts any light source into energy to power the watch. Founded in 1918, Citizen produces a wide range of watches from affordable everyday timepieces to the high-end Chronomaster collection. The brand also manufactures watch movements used by many other brands worldwide.

name: Casio
description: Casio Computer Co., Ltd. is a Japanese multinational electronics company. In watchmaking, Casio is renowned for affordable, durable, and feature-rich digital watches. The company produces everything from the iconic F-91W to sophisticated GPS and Bluetooth-connected timepieces.

name: G-Shock
description: G-Shock is a line of watches manufactured by Casio, designed to resist mechanical shock and vibration. Created by engineer Kikuo Ibe in 1983, G-Shock watches are built with a hollow structure that suspends the module within the case. The brand has become a cultural icon, popular in military, law enforcement, extreme sports, and street fashion.

name: Hublot
description: Hublot is a Swiss luxury watchmaker founded in 1980 by Carlo Crocco. The brand is known for its 'Art of Fusion' philosophy, combining unconventional materials like ceramic, carbon fiber, and sapphire crystal in bold designs. The Big Bang collection, launched in 2005 under CEO Jean-Claude Biver, transformed Hublot into one of the fastest-growing luxury watch brands.

name: Zenith
description: Zenith is a Swiss luxury watchmaker founded in 1865 in Le Locle. Famous for the El Primero movement — the world's first automatic chronograph calibre, beating at 36,000 vph — Zenith has been at the forefront of high-frequency watchmaking. Rolex used the El Primero as the base for the Daytona chronograph for over 30 years.

name: Panerai
description: Officine Panerai is an Italian luxury watchmaker originally founded in Florence. Initially a supplier of precision instruments to the Italian Navy, Panerai's military heritage defines its aesthetic: large cushion cases, luminous dials, and crown-protecting bridge devices. The Luminor and Radiomir remain among the most recognizable watch designs in the world.

name: Vacheron Constantin
description: Vacheron Constantin is a Swiss luxury watchmaker and one of the oldest watch manufacturers in the world, with an uninterrupted history since 1755. Part of the 'Holy Trinity' of watchmaking alongside Patek Philippe and Audemars Piguet, Vacheron Constantin is known for the Overseas, Patrimony, and Historiques collections, as well as extraordinary custom pieces through its Les Cabinotiers atelier.

name: A. Lange & Söhne
description: A. Lange & Söhne is a German luxury watchmaker based in Glashütte, Saxony. Founded by Ferdinand Adolph Lange in 1845, the company was expropriated after World War II and refounded by Walter Lange in 1990 after German reunification. Known for meticulous German engineering, silver and gold movement finishing, and the iconic outsize date, Lange produces only a few thousand watches per year.

name: Hamilton
description: Hamilton Watch Company is an American-Swiss watchmaker founded in Lancaster, Pennsylvania. Known as the 'Watch of Railroad Accuracy,' Hamilton has appeared in over 500 Hollywood films. Now headquartered in Biel/Bienne and part of the Swatch Group, Hamilton offers Swiss-made watches with American design DNA at accessible price points.

name: Orient
description: Orient Watch Co. is a Japanese watchmaker and subsidiary of Seiko Epson. Known for producing affordable mechanical watches with in-house movements, Orient offers remarkable value. The Bambino dress watch and Mako/Ray dive watches are considered some of the best entry-level mechanical watches available.

name: Bulgari
description: Bulgari is an Italian luxury brand founded in Rome in 1884. Known primarily for jewellery, Bulgari has made significant contributions to watchmaking with the Octo Finissimo collection, which has broken multiple records for the world's thinnest watches. The Serpenti and Lvcea collections showcase Bulgari's distinctive Italian design aesthetic.

name: Breitling
description: Breitling SA is a Swiss luxury watchmaker known for precision-made chronographs and its strong connection to aviation. Founded in 1884, Breitling developed the modern chronograph pushpiece and the Navitimer with its iconic slide rule bezel. The brand is now owned by CVC Capital Partners and produces robust, pilot-oriented timepieces.

name: NOMOS Glashütte
description: NOMOS Glashütte is a German watch manufacturer based in Glashütte, Saxony. Founded in 1990 shortly after German reunification, NOMOS is known for Bauhaus-inspired minimalist designs and in-house movements. Despite being a relatively young brand, NOMOS has earned recognition for producing high-quality, well-designed watches at reasonable prices.

name: Rado
description: Rado is a Swiss watchmaker known as the 'Master of Materials.' A pioneer in the use of high-tech ceramics, hardened metals, and sapphire crystal in watchmaking, Rado created the world's first scratch-resistant watch in 1962. The brand continues to push material science boundaries with plasma high-tech ceramic and ceramos.

name: Frederique Constant
description: Frederique Constant is a Swiss watchmaker based in Geneva. Founded in 1988, the brand's mission is to make luxury Swiss watches accessible to a broader audience. Known for the Classics and Highlife collections, Frederique Constant produces in-house movements and offers exceptional value in the accessible luxury segment.

name: Bulova
description: Bulova Corporation is an American watchmaker founded in New York City. Known for innovation, Bulova produced the first fully electronic watch (Accutron, 1960) using a tuning fork mechanism, and the Accutron technology was used in NASA instruments and satellites. Now part of the Citizen Group, Bulova offers affordable watches with notable heritage.

name: Piaget
description: Piaget is a Swiss luxury watchmaker and jeweller founded in La Côte-aux-Fées. Known as a pioneer of ultra-thin watchmaking, Piaget has held multiple records for the world's thinnest movements and watches. The Altiplano and Polo collections showcase the brand's mastery of slim elegance, while jewelled timepieces display extraordinary gem-setting craftsmanship.

name: Titan
description: Titan Company Limited is an Indian consumer goods company and a subsidiary of the Tata Group, headquartered in Bengaluru. Founded in 1984 as a joint venture between the Tata Group and the Tamil Nadu Industrial Development Corporation, Titan is the fifth-largest integrated own-brand watch manufacturer in the world. Known for collections like Edge (world's slimmest ceramic watch at 4.4mm), Octane, Raga, and Nebula, Titan operates over 2,000 retail stores across India.

name: HMT
description: Hindustan Machine Tools (HMT) Watches was India's first watchmaker, established in 1961 in Bangalore through a technology transfer with Citizen of Japan. Known as the 'Timekeeper to the Nation,' HMT produced iconic models like the Janata, Pilot, and Sona that became symbols of Indian self-reliance. At its peak in the 1980s, HMT was the largest watch supplier in India. The watch division closed in 2016, and HMT watches are now highly valued collectibles.

name: Delhi Watch Company
description: Delhi Watch Company is an Indian microbrand founded in 2020 that celebrates Delhi's cultural heritage through watchmaking. Starting with the Dilli 36 collection featuring designs inspired by 36 iconic Delhi neighborhoods, DWC focuses on affordable, handcrafted timepieces using locally sourced materials. Each watch incorporates engraved casebacks and dial textures that pay tribute to Delhi's layered history.

name: Coromandel Watch Co.
description: Coromandel Watch Co. is an Indian microbrand founded in 2024 that manufactures high-quality handcrafted watches blending traditional South Indian heritage with modern design. Named after the Coromandel Coast, the brand has released collections including the Blue Mountain Edition (tribute to the Nilgiris), Tanjore Edition (inspired by Thanjavur art), and the Varnam Collection featuring dials inspired by the Western Ghats.

name: Blancpain
description: Blancpain is the oldest surviving watchmaking brand in the world. Known for the iconic Fifty Fathoms diver and the elegant Villeret collection, Blancpain has never produced a quartz watch.

name: Breguet
description: Founded by Abraham-Louis Breguet, one of the most important horologists in history. Breguet invented the tourbillon, perpetual calendar, and many other complications. The brand remains synonymous with haute horlogerie.

name: Oris
description: Oris is one of the few remaining independent Swiss watch manufacturers. Known for accessible mechanical watches, particularly the Aquis dive watch and vintage-inspired Divers Sixty-Five.
